course.title: HTTP Clients in Go 1.26: Reliable Integrations
course.description: Build secure, bounded, observable Go HTTP clients from protocol semantics through redirects, streaming, retries, connection reuse, testing, and production defense. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate http semantics through rfc 9110, caching, http/1.1, http/2, http/3, uri resolution, problem details, cookies, https, redirects, credentials, streaming, conditional requests, retries, rate limits, pagination, and observability through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate go 1.26 net/http request construction, response handling, runtime validation, cancellation, testing seams, resource ownership, and production transfer evidence through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate cumulative projects spanning protocol reasoning, safe destination policy, bounded resources, fault injection, accessibility, privacy, performance, release gates, and production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
